
---
title: "Hunga-Tonga's eruption and its impact on the weather and the environment"
subtitle: "project 2"
author: "Paweł Stefańczyk , Przemysław Kania"
institute: "MiNI PW"
date: "10.06.2024"
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    lib_dir: libs
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
---

```{r, include=FALSE}
ham_pres <- read.csv("C:\\Users\\przem\\OneDrive\\Pulpit\\PRZEMO\\STUDIES_MINI_PW\\SEM__2__\\R\\Homework_2\\csv_files\\csv_files_for_pressure_data\\Hammamatsu_pressure.csv")
kas_pres <- read.csv("C:\\Users\\przem\\OneDrive\\Pulpit\\PRZEMO\\STUDIES_MINI_PW\\SEM__2__\\R\\Homework_2\\csv_files\\csv_files_for_pressure_data\\Kasprowy_Wierch_pressure.csv")
gil_pres <- read.csv("C:\\Users\\przem\\OneDrive\\Pulpit\\PRZEMO\\STUDIES_MINI_PW\\SEM__2__\\R\\Homework_2\\csv_files\\csv_files_for_pressure_data\\Gilsborne_pressure.csv")
bos_pres <- read.csv("C:\\Users\\przem\\OneDrive\\Pulpit\\PRZEMO\\STUDIES_MINI_PW\\SEM__2__\\R\\Homework_2\\csv_files\\csv_files_for_pressure_data\\Boston_pressure.csv")
wulkan <- read.csv("C:\\Users\\przem\\OneDrive\\Pulpit\\PRZEMO\\STUDIES_MINI_PW\\SEM__2__\\R\\Homework_2\\csv_files\\csv_files_for_air_pollution_data\\wulkan.csv")
wyspa_tonga <- read.csv("C:\\Users\\przem\\OneDrive\\Pulpit\\PRZEMO\\STUDIES_MINI_PW\\SEM__2__\\R\\Homework_2\\csv_files\\csv_files_for_air_pollution_data\\wyspa_tonga1.csv")
Hunga_hapai <- read.csv("C:\\Users\\przem\\OneDrive\\Pulpit\\PRZEMO\\STUDIES_MINI_PW\\SEM__2__\\R\\Homework_2\\csv_files\\csv_files_for_air_pollution_data\\Hunga_hapai1.csv")

library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)
library(knitr)
library(xaringan)
```


# O WULKANIE

+ wulkan podwodny

+ 65 km od wybrzeży tongatapu, największego archipelagu wysp Tonga

+ erupcja 15 stycznia 2022 około godziny 15:30

+ erupcjom wulkanów zawsze towarzyszą zmiany w atmosferze i środowisku

![](Hunga_Tonga_volcano.jpg)
---

```{r, echo=FALSE, out.width='90%'}
       ####### BOSTON #######
bos_pres$time <- as.POSIXct(bos_pres$time, format = "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M")
mean_bos_pressure <- mean(bos_pres$pressure_hPa)
gg_bos <- ggplot(bos_pres, aes(x = time, y = pressure_hPa)) + 
    xlab("TIME (January)") + ylab("PRESSURE (hPa)") + 
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(1010,1040), breaks = seq(1010, 1040, by = 5), expand = c(0.1, 0.1)) +
    scale_x_datetime(breaks = seq(as.POSIXct("2022-01-14"), as.POSIXct("2022-01-17"), by = "6 hours"), 
        date_labels = "%H:%M", expand = c(0.05, 0.05)) + theme_bw() + geom_line(color = "#FF00FF") +
    scale_linetype_manual(values = "solid") +
    ggtitle("BOSTON 2022", subtitle = "USA") +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold",color = "blue", hjust = 0.5),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, face = "plain", color = "blue", hjust = 0.5),
        axis.text.x = element_text(color="black"), axis.text.y = element_text(color="black"),
        panel.background = element_rect(color="black", fill="white")) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = mean_bos_pressure, color = "blue", linetype = "dashed") 
ggplotly(gg_bos)
```


--> nagła zmiana ciśnienia atmosferycznego i tworzenie się tym samym fali uderzeniowej, która osiągnęła prędkość około 1200km/h (dane zebrane na      przedziale czasowym (14.01.2022 - 16.01.2022) <--

---

```{r, echo=FALSE, out.width='90%'}
       ####### GILSBORNE #######
gil_pres$time <- as.POSIXct(gil_pres$time, format = "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M")
mean_gil_pressure <- mean(gil_pres$pressure_hPa)
gg_gil <- ggplot(gil_pres, aes(x = time, y = pressure_hPa)) + 
  xlab("TIME (January)") + ylab("PRESSURE (hPa)") + 
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(1005,1035), breaks = seq(1005, 1035, by = 5), expand = c(0.1, 0.1)) +
  scale_x_datetime(breaks = seq(as.POSIXct("2022-01-14"), as.POSIXct("2022-01-17"), by = "6 hours"), 
        date_labels = "%H:%M", expand = c(0.05, 0.05)) + theme_bw() + geom_line(color = "#FF00FF") +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = "solid") +
  ggtitle("GILSBORNE 2022", subtitle = "New Zeland") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold",color = "blue", hjust = 0.5),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, face = "plain", color = "blue", hjust = 0.5),
        axis.text.x = element_text(color="black"), axis.text.y = element_text(color="black"),
        panel.background = element_rect(color="black", fill="white")) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = mean_gil_pressure, color = "blue", linetype = "dashed") 
ggplotly(gg_gil)
```

---

```{r, echo=FALSE, out.width='90%'}
       ####### HAMMAMATSU #######
ham_pres$time <- as.POSIXct(ham_pres$time, format = "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M")
mean_ham_pressure <- mean(ham_pres$pressure_hPa)
gg_ham <- ggplot(ham_pres, aes(x = time, y = pressure_hPa)) + 
  xlab("TIME (January)") + ylab("PRESSURE (hPa)") + 
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(980,1025), breaks = seq(980, 1025, by = 5), expand = c(0.1, 0.1)) +
  scale_x_datetime(breaks = seq(as.POSIXct("2022-01-14"), as.POSIXct("2022-01-17"), by = "6 hours"), 
        date_labels = "%H:%M", expand = c(0.05, 0.05)) + theme_bw() + geom_line(color = "#FF00FF") +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = "solid") +
  ggtitle("HAMMAMATSU 2022", subtitle = "Japan") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold",color = "blue", hjust = 0.5),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, face = "plain", color = "blue", hjust = 0.5),
        axis.text.x = element_text(color="black"), axis.text.y = element_text(color="black"),
        panel.background = element_rect(color="black", fill="white")) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = mean_ham_pressure, color = "blue", linetype = "dashed") 
ggplotly(gg_ham)
```

---

```{r, echo=FALSE, out.width='90%'}
       ####### KASPROWY WIERCH #######
kas_pres$time <- as.POSIXct(kas_pres$time, format = "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M")
mean_kas_pressure <- mean(kas_pres$pressure_hPa)
gg_kas <- ggplot(kas_pres, aes(x = time, y = pressure_hPa)) + 
  xlab("TIME (January)") + ylab("PRESSURE (hPa)") + 
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(1010,1040), breaks = seq(1010, 1040, by = 5), expand = c(0.1, 0.1)) +
  scale_x_datetime(breaks = seq(as.POSIXct("2022-01-14"), as.POSIXct("2022-01-17"), by = "6 hours"), 
        date_labels = "%H:%M", expand = c(0.05, 0.05)) + theme_bw() + geom_line(color = "#FF00FF") +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = "solid") +
  ggtitle("KASPROWY WIERCH 2022", subtitle = "Poland") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold",color = "blue", hjust = 0.5),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, face = "plain", color = "blue", hjust = 0.5),
        axis.text.x = element_text(color="black"), axis.text.y = element_text(color="black"),
        panel.background = element_rect(color="black", fill="white")) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = mean_kas_pressure, color = "blue", linetype = "dashed") 
ggplotly(gg_kas)
```

---

```{r, echo=FALSE, out.width='90%', out.height='85%'}
bos_max_value <- max(bos_pres$pressure_hPa)
 max_bos_index <- which(bos_pres$pressure_hPa == bos_max_value)
 bos_prev_value <- bos_pres[max_bos_index - 1, "pressure_hPa"]
 bos_difference <- bos_max_value - bos_prev_value
gil_max_value <- max(gil_pres$pressure_hPa)
 max_gil_index <- which(gil_pres$pressure_hPa == gil_max_value)
 gil_prev_value <- gil_pres[max_gil_index - 1, "pressure_hPa"]
 gil_difference <- gil_max_value - gil_prev_value
ham_max_value <- max(ham_pres$pressure_hPa)
 max_ham_index <- which(ham_pres$pressure_hPa == ham_max_value)
 ham_prev_value <- ham_pres[max_ham_index - 1, "pressure_hPa"]
 ham_difference <- ham_max_value - ham_prev_value
kas_max_value <- max(kas_pres$pressure_hPa)
 max_kas_index <- which(kas_pres$pressure_hPa == kas_max_value)
 kas_prev_value <- kas_pres[max_kas_index - 1, "pressure_hPa"]
 kas_difference <- kas_max_value - kas_prev_value
all_diff <- data.frame(city = c("BOSTON","GILSBORNE","HAMMAMATSU","KASPROWY WIERCH"), pressure_diff = c(bos_difference,gil_difference,ham_difference,kas_difference))
gg_pres_difference <- ggplot(all_diff, aes(x = city, y = pressure_diff)) +
  geom_bar(width = 1, stat = "identity") + theme_bw() + coord_polar("x", start = 1) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Pastel1") + ggtitle("PRESSURE DIFFERENCE") +
  theme(axis.line = element_blank(), axis.ticks = element_blank(),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold",color = "blue", hjust = 0.5)) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(pressure_diff, 1), " hPa")), position = position_stack(vjust = 0.75)) 
gg_pres_difference
```

---

# Ciekawostki

+ erupcja była w przybliżeniu 500 razy silniejsza niż wybuch bomby atomowej zrzuconej na Hiroszimę 

+ wybuchowi towarzyszyły wyładowania atmosferyczne; w ciągu 6h zarejerstrowano około 400 000 wulkanicznych błyskawic

+ wytworzyło się tsunami o falach osiągających zatrważające wysokości nawet 2m

+ erupcja ta, chociaż uznawana za najpotężniejszą od ponad 30 lat, zabiła zaledwie 6 osób

+ z racji tego, że do wybuchu doszło pod wodami Oceanu Spokojnego, wytworzony słup pyłu składał się w ponad 60% (około 150 mln ton) z pary wodnej   i wzniósł się na wysokość około 55 km

---

# O dwutlenku siarki w powietrzu

+ znacząco ogranicza proces fotosyntezy

+ niszczy bakterie glebowe, przyczynia się do wzrostu kwaśnych deszczy

+ zbyt wysoki poziom tego związku ma dewastacyjny wpływ na pracę układu oddechowego

---

```{r, echo=FALSE, out.width='95%'}
wulkan$TIME <- as.Date(wulkan$TIME)
normal_sulphur_intensity <- mean(wulkan$SULPHUR.DIOXIDE)
standard_deviation <- sd(wulkan$SULPHUR.DIOXIDE)
max_spike <- which.max(wulkan$SULPHUR.DIOXIDE - normal_sulphur_intensity)
t <- ggplot(wulkan, aes(x = TIME, y = SULPHUR.DIOXIDE)) + xlab("CZAS") + ylab("DWUTLENEK SIARKI µg/m³") +
  geom_line() +
  geom_hline(yintercept = normal_sulphur_intensity, color = "turquoise4", linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = normal_sulphur_intensity - standard_deviation, ymax = normal_sulphur_intensity + standard_deviation), alpha = 0.6, colour = "#FFA07A", fill = "#CD8162") +
  ggtitle("ILOSC DWUTLENKU SIARKI W POWIETRZU (WULKAN)") + 
  theme(plot.title = element_text(colour = "green", hjust = 0.5, size = 16)) + 
  theme(plot.background = element_rect(fill = "#E0FFFF", colour = "#E0FFFF")) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 15), breaks = seq(0, 15, by = 1.25))
ggplotly(t)
```

--> Dane przedstawione na powyższych wykresach obejmują okres od poczatku sierpnia do poczatku maja
(04.08.2021 - 01.05.2022) <--

---

```{r, echo=FALSE, out.width='95%'}
wyspa_tonga$TIME <- as.Date(wyspa_tonga$TIME)
normal_sulphur_intensity1 <- mean(wyspa_tonga$SULPHUR.DIOXIDE)
standard_deviation1 <- sd(wyspa_tonga$SULPHUR.DIOXIDE)
z <- ggplot(wyspa_tonga, aes(x = TIME, y = SULPHUR.DIOXIDE)) + xlab("CZAS") + ylab("DWUTLENEK SIARKI µg/m³") +
  geom_line() + 
  geom_hline(yintercept = normal_sulphur_intensity1, color = "turquoise4", linetype = "dashed") + 
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = normal_sulphur_intensity1 - standard_deviation1, ymax = normal_sulphur_intensity1 + standard_deviation1), alpha = 0.6, colour = "#FFA07A", fill = "#CD8162") +
  ggtitle("ILOSC DWUTLENKU SIARKI W POWIETRZU (TONGA)") + 
  theme(plot.title = element_text(colour = "green", hjust = 0.5, size = 16)) + 
  theme(plot.background = element_rect(fill = "#E0FFFF", colour = "#E0FFFF")) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 15), breaks = seq(0, 15, by = 1.25))
ggplotly(z)
```

---

```{r, echo=FALSE, out.width='95%'}
Hunga_hapai$TIME <- as.Date(Hunga_hapai$TIME)
normal_sulphur_intensity2 <- mean(Hunga_hapai$SULPHUR.DIOXIDE)
standard_deviation2 <- sd(Hunga_hapai$SULPHUR.DIOXIDE)
k <- ggplot(Hunga_hapai, aes(x = TIME, y = SULPHUR.DIOXIDE)) + xlab("CZAS") + ylab("DWUTLENEK SIARKI µg/m³") +
  geom_line() +
  geom_hline(yintercept = normal_sulphur_intensity2, color = "turquoise4", linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = normal_sulphur_intensity2 - standard_deviation2, ymax = normal_sulphur_intensity2 + standard_deviation2), alpha = 0.6, colour = "#FFA07A", fill = "#CD8162") +
  ggtitle("ILOSC DWUTLENKU SIARKI W POWIETRZU (HUNGA)") + 
  theme(plot.title = element_text(colour = "green", hjust = 0.5, size = 16)) + 
  theme(plot.background = element_rect(fill = "#E0FFFF", colour = "#E0FFFF")) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 15), breaks = seq(0, 15, by = 1.25))
ggplotly(k)
```

---

```{r, echo=FALSE, out.width='95%'}
islands_names <- c("Wulkan", "Tonga", "Hunga")
srednie <- data.frame(Wyspa = islands_names, wartosci = c(normal_sulphur_intensity, normal_sulphur_intensity1, normal_sulphur_intensity2))  
p <- ggplot(srednie, aes(x = islands_names, y = wartosci)) + 
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#6B8E23", width = 0.4, colour = "blue") +
  labs(x = "Nazwy Wysp", y = "Srednia siarka µg/m³") + 
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1.5), breaks = seq(0, 1.5, by = 0.1)) + 
  ggtitle("SREDNIA ILOSC DWUTLENKU SIARKI NA WYSPACH") + 
  theme(plot.title = element_text(colour = "#EE9A00", hjust = 0.5, size = 16)) +
  theme(plot.background = element_rect(fill = "#E0FFFF",colour = "#E0FFFF"))
ggplotly(p)
```

---

```{r, echo=FALSE, out.width='95%'}
wulkan$Year <- format(wulkan$TIME, "%Y")
wulkan$Month <- format(wulkan$TIME, "%m")
srednie_miesiace <- aggregate(wulkan$SULPHUR.DIOXIDE, by = wulkan[c("Year", "Month")], mean)
ord <- order(srednie_miesiace$Year, srednie_miesiace$Month)
srednie_miesiace <- srednie_miesiace[ord, ]
srednie_miesiace$MonthName <- c("Aug", "Sep", "Oct", "Nov", "Dec", "Jan", "Feb", "Mar", "Apr", "May")
srednie_miesiace$MonthName <- factor(srednie_miesiace$MonthName, levels = c("Aug", "Sep", "Oct", "Nov", "Dec", "Jan", "Feb", "Mar", "Apr", "May"))
colnames(srednie_miesiace)[3] <- "Srednia"
s <- ggplot(srednie_miesiace, aes(x = MonthName, y = Srednia)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#76EEC6", width = 0.1, colour = "#458B74") +
  labs(x = "CZAS", y = "Srednia siarka w miesiacu µg/m³") +
  ggtitle("Srednie wartosi dwutlenku siarki przy wulkanie") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(colour = "#8B4500", hjust = 0.5, size = 16)) +
  theme(plot.background = element_rect(colour = "#E0FFFF", fill = "#E0FFFF")) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 3), breaks = seq(0, 3, by = 0.25))
ggplotly(s)
```

---

# Wnioski

+ Wybuch wulkanu spowodował wzrost poziomu dwutlenku siarki w promieniu co najmniej 5 km

+ Faza uśpienia wulkanu również ma wpływ na duży wzrost dwutlenku siarki w powietrzu

+ Dwutlenek siarki może utrzymywać się w powietrzu przez ponad miesiąc

---

class: center, middle, inverse
#Dziękujemy za uwagę


### Sources

+ https://pl.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hunga_Tonga
+ https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=S-qv_GzGt1k&t=320s
+ https://open-meteo.com/
